text: I wish I had started earlier, but I am proud of every milestone.
Although the road was long, this is just one more beginning.

Sandra, twenty lessons! Tell me, how did your journey begin?
At the beginning, I only knew a few words, and I was shy.
And was there a turning point that changed things?
Yes. The turning point was my first full conversation. I still recall it, and I am grateful.
Beautiful. Do you have any regret, looking back?
I wish I had started earlier. Although the road was long, this is just one more chapter.

Let me tell you the story of my own language journey. The beginning was not easy. Years ago, I could only say a few words, and I was afraid to speak. If I had given up then, I would not be here today. But I kept going. The turning point came when I read my grandmother's old letters. That memory still makes me grateful. Although progress was slow, every small milestone felt huge. I remember the first time I told a long story without stopping. I wish I had started earlier, but I do not waste time on regret. When I recall those years, I feel proud. The lesson of my journey is simple. Your story is never finished. There is always a new chapter, a new word, a new beginning. So keep telling your story, one sentence at a time.

I want to tell you my own English story so far. The beginning feels far away now. At first, I only knew a handful of words, and I was a little shy. If someone had told me I would speak this much, I would not have believed them. The turning point was the day I decided that age was not a wall. I am fifty-one, and I am proud of every milestone. I recall my first full conversation, and I still feel grateful for it. Although the road was long, I never gave up. I wish I had more free time to study, but I make the most of every lesson. When I look back, I have very few regrets. Each new chapter of my journey opens a door. This is not the end. It is one more beginning, and I am ready for the next part of my story.

First, set the beginning of your story, for example when I started, I knew very little. Then, describe the turning point, the moment that changed something, like the day I decided to keep going. Next, add a wish or a small regret, such as I wish I had started earlier. After that, name a milestone you are proud of, for example my first full conversation. Finally, close with hope, like this is just one more chapter, and I am ready for the next.
